intro: Gothofreda pachyglossa, also known as Annona glabra, is a species of flowering plant in the custard apple family (Annonaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in treating various ailments such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Gothofreda pachyglossa include annonin, which has been shown to exhibit significant pharmacological activities, and other compounds such as alkaloids and saponins. In traditional herbal medicine, Gothofreda pachyglossa is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams to treat various health conditions.
